repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: finite null-annihilator/row-space criterion for proximal ATE point identification

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023: proximal counterfactual means can be identified without unique bridge functions
BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 and SmuclerRobinsRotnitzky2025 are closest; neither gives a proximal finite-dictionary rho_n iff frontier
SmuclerRobinsRotnitzky2025, Santos2012, Hong2017, and AndrewsCheng2012 are closest; none gives the finite proximal Q_n projection frontier indexed by rho_n

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Antoine and Lavergne (2014, Journal of Econometrics), which directly studies semi-strong identification in conditional moment models with flattening conditional moments and a robust minimum-distance estimator; it does not subsume the proximal rho_n claim, but it is a closer comparator than AndrewsCheng2012 alone.

soundness_flags.label: Strong-proxy reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: The claim rho_n(P_n) asymp sqrt(n) needs the certificate norm lambda' [T' Omega^{-1} T]^+ lambda bounded away from both zero and infinity; a bounded-below singular value plus bounded lambda only gives an upper bound and allows lambda to shrink or vanish.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add AntoineLavergne2014 to Related work and the Conjecture 1 why-new block, then state explicitly that their conditional-moment semi-strong-ID rates do not construct the proximal target-loading certificate rho_n or the bridge-envelope endpoint.
Patch the strong-proxy corollary by assuming 0 < c <= lambda' [T' Omega^{-1} T]^+ lambda <= C < infinity, or weaken the conclusion to rho_n at least of order sqrt(n).
For the witness, include the closed-form limiting projection interval endpoints after eliminating strong coordinates, so the nonshrinking-diameter claim is auditable without relying on prose.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison and the strong-proxy corollary; the flagship kernel otherwise survives both repo and published novelty checks.
